name: localshop
last_updated: 2022-07 (last commit to master 2022-07-17; setup.py at 2.0.0-alpha.1; last PyPI release 0.9.3 was 2015-05-15)
perf_notes: No published benchmarks. Standard Django/gunicorn WSGI app with a Celery worker for background mirroring. On-demand proxy: the first request for an un-cached file returns a 302 redirect to PyPI while the worker downloads and caches it, so initial latency is dominated by the PyPI fetch. With LOCALSHOP_ISOLATED=True there is no redirect and the client blocks until the download completes (docs recommend raising PIP_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT, e.g. 300s).
scale_notes: Stateless web tier (gunicorn) can run as multiple instances behind a load balancer with a shared PostgreSQL DB, shared Redis broker, and shared file storage (filesystem/S3). A Celery worker handles background mirror downloads and celery-beat acts as a singleton scheduler. No built-in HA/clustering, replication, health, or readiness features; scaling is conventional Django scaling. LOCALSHOP_USE_PROXIED_IP supports running behind a reverse proxy (X-Forwarded-For). Project is effectively unmaintained (last commit 2022).
summary: Django-based self-hosted private PyPI server that lazily proxies and caches PyPI on demand, hosts private packages across multiple indexes, and gates access by team membership and CIDR/IP allowlists.
extras: On-demand 302-redirect proxy: an un-cached file request redirects the client straight to PyPI while a Celery worker downloads and caches it for next time (stored under ~/.localshop/files)
LOCALSHOP_ISOLATED mode for air-gapped/offline operation: never redirect to PyPI; download-then-serve
CIDR/IP-based access permissions configured per index in the web UI (set 0.0.0.0/0 to allow any IP); X-Forwarded-For aware via LOCALSHOP_USE_PROXIED_IP
Access-key/secret-key credential pairs (API-token style) used over HTTP Basic for both download and upload
Configurable version-scheme enforcement (PEP 440 / Simple3/Simple4/Perl schemes via the Versio library) through LOCALSHOP_VERSIONING_TYPE
Immutable releases via LOCALSHOP_RELEASE_OVERWRITE=False (mirrors PyPI's no-overwrite behaviour); LOCALSHOP_DELETE_FILES cleans files on package/release delete
Pluggable Django storage backends (default OverwriteStorage on local filesystem; S3 via django-storages/boto3; GCS/Azure possible via other django-storages backends)
OAuth2 social login (e.g. Azure AD) via social-auth-app-django; users and teams managed through the Django /admin backend; only teams (not individual users) are assigned to repositories
Queries upstream PyPI via the XML-RPC interface to discover packages (XML-RPC used as a client, not exposed to consumers)
License is MIT per the repository LICENSE file (and GitHub detection); the setup.py 'License :: BSD' classifier is stale/incorrect metadata
Largely unmaintained: Python 2-era project migrated to Django 2.2/Py3; master sits at 2.0.0-alpha.1, last commit 2022, last PyPI release (0.9.3) in 2015
